summary: Iteration 5: Frontend-only regression testing of ErrorBoundary + safe access + relative-API fallback for AUTOHAUS. All 7 spec items pass. Landing (/) renders full DOM (hero-section, hero-headline='ПРЕОБРАЖАЕМ АВТОМОБИЛИ В ПРОИЗВЕДЕНИЕ ИСКУССТВА', main-nav, brand-logo='AUTOHAUS KALININGRAD', nav-cta-button, hero-cta-primary, landing-page container). Zero PAGE ERRORS, zero console errors/warnings. Full-page scroll (16183px height) triggers no runtime errors. All 9 landing sections present: protocol, quiz, before-after (via data-testid=before-after-section), services, configurator, stats (via data-testid=stats-section), gallery, live, contact + footer. ErrorBoundary component NOT triggered on / or /admin/login (checked [data-testid=global-error-boundary] absent). Clicking [data-testid=nav-cta-button] opens LeadPopup with WhatsApp + Telegram messenger icons visible. /admin/login renders form (email/password/submit) with no black screen and no console errors. Admin login with admin@detailing-autohaus.ru / AutohausAdmin2026! successfully redirects to /admin CMS panel showing 'AUTOHAUS · CMS' + Hero editing form + 'Вход выполнен' toast.
critical_code_review_comments: ErrorBoundary is only wrapping the top-level tree; it will catch any child render error and show the fallback, but it does NOT catch errors thrown inside useEffect or async callbacks (React limitation). If a section fetches from `${API}/...` and the fetch handler throws, only console.error will fire. ContentContext already handles axios failures via try/catch, so this is safe today, but adding per-section boundaries around heavy sections (Configurator, AutoHausLive) would improve resilience if E1 later adds risky async logic.

Hero.jsx uses `content?.hero || {}` safe access — good. However other sections (Services, Configurator, Gallery, SafetyProtocol, Quiz, ContactForm, Footer, AutoHausLive, BeforeAfter, Stats) still rely on `content.<section>` direct access via useContent(). Since FALLBACK_CONTENT + deepMerge in ContentContext guarantee those keys always exist (initial state = FALLBACK_CONTENT, never null), this works today, but a future ContentProvider bug that sets content to null/undefined would still trigger PAGE ERROR in those sections despite ErrorBoundary catching it. Consider applying the same `content?.section || {}` pattern globally for full defense-in-depth.

ContentContext.jsx BACKEND fallback = '' with API = '/api' works for same-domain nginx proxy (VPS). However in dev / preview builds the REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is baked into the JS bundle at build time — verified working on preview URL (https://luxury-wrap.preview.emergentagent.com). Ensure the VPS production build is done WITH REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL unset OR set to '' explicitly, so the relative /api path is used and nginx proxies it. Document this in deployment README.

ErrorBoundary.handleReload does window.location.reload() — if the error is deterministic (e.g. missing content field), the reload will loop the user back to the fallback screen. Consider adding a secondary 'Вернуться на главную' link to /?safe=1 that could skip the failing section, or track hasError count in sessionStorage to prevent infinite loops.
test_credentials: admin@detailing-autohaus.ru / AutohausAdmin2026! — verified working via UI login → /admin redirect
seed_data_creation: None. No leads or content mutations were performed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Preview URL https://luxury-wrap.preview.emergentagent.com/ renders landing + admin flows cleanly with the new ErrorBoundary + Hero safe-access + relative /api fallback. Zero runtime errors on both / and /admin/login. Admin CMS panel fully functional (Hero form + sidebar sections: Hero, Before/After, Протокол, Услуги, Конфигуратор, Цифры, Работы, AutoHaus Live, Квиз, Exit-Intent, Контакты, Подвал). The previously-reported 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading poster_url)' bug is NOT reproducible on the preview build — Hero.jsx now safely handles content?.hero || {} + resolveMedia fallback. Note: BeforeAfter section uses data-testid=before-after-section (NOT id=before-after) and Stats uses data-testid=stats-section (NOT id=stats) — testing agents should use data-testid selectors, not #id, for these two sections.
rca of the issue: n/a — no issues found. Previous poster_url PAGE ERROR was resolved by the three-part fix: (1) ErrorBoundary wrap in App.js catches any residual render error → shows soft fallback instead of black screen; (2) Hero.jsx `content?.hero || {}` prevents undefined access at first render before ContentContext resolves; (3) ContentContext + adminApi + contentDefaults fallback to '' + relative /api so the same JS bundle works on VPS (nginx proxy) and Vercel/preview (absolute URL from env). Verified end-to-end on preview build.
